title: Map and station travel clarity pass
summary: Reduce friction between world map, field station, adjacent walking, and route markers now that the alpha arc is closed.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Travel intent is clear from map/station/route surfaces, and no surface points to completed High Pass as active work.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Audit and harden map/station focus, return targets, route markers, and filed-state travel suppression.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Clarify travel labels and tiny route destination copy without adding a map tutorial paragraph.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Polish one map/door/return visual cue only where proof shows spatial confusion.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Assert route marker/support behavior across active, ready, filed, replay, and no-active-route states.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

lane_1_scout_refinement.target: Add one focused test-only regression proving filed High Pass cannot revive active travel cues when route-marker remains selected.
existing_coverage: Filed High Pass chapter state already sets routeBoardTargetBiomeId to null and isActiveOuting to false.
Field-request state already requires an active outing before route-marker focus or replay labels can appear.
The high-pass-filed debug save snapshot covers filed station/map/journal state with the default support selection.
A broad runtime season-capstone smoke reaches route-marker-selected active High Pass, ready-to-file High Pass, and filed High Pass, but it is not a focused stale-support regression.
recommended_scope: Build the existing high-pass-filed debug snapshot save, set selectedOutingSupportId to route-marker, boot it through createGame(), and assert the filed state stays settled.
Use a focused runtime-smoke seed only if the snapshot helper shape blocks the small save-snapshot proof.
acceptance: Filed High Pass with route-marker selected has activeFieldRequest null.
Opening the world map keeps routeMarkerLocationId null and routeReplayLabel null; Today: High Pass must not reappear.
Opening the journal keeps journal.fieldRequest null.
The field station route board remains filed with targetBiomeId null, the filed nextDirection, and a filed launch card.
Activating the filed High Pass expedition card stays in the station and shows the filed/logged notice instead of opening travel.

result: Added a focused save-snapshot regression that boots filed High Pass with selectedOutingSupportId set to route-marker.
Proved filed High Pass keeps activeFieldRequest null, routeMarkerLocationId null, routeReplayLabel null, and journal.fieldRequest null.
Proved the filed route board remains targetless/filed and the expedition card activation stays in the station with the filed/logged notice instead of opening travel.

lane_1_critic_result.result: Clean review. The filed High Pass stale-route-marker regression proves completed-arc travel cues stay suppressed without changing runtime behavior.
confirmed: The test mutates only a copied high-pass-filed debug snapshot save by setting selectedOutingSupportId to route-marker.
Filed station route board state stays targetless, complete, and filed while the selected support remains route-marker.
World-map activeFieldRequest, routeMarkerLocationId, and routeReplayLabel stay null.
Filed High Pass expedition activation stays in the station with the filed/logged notice instead of opening travel.

lane_2_scout_refinement.target: Clarify the Treeline Pass map footer and High Pass active-route summary so place and route language do not blur together.

recommended_copy.src/content/world-map.ts treeline.summar: Wind-bent edge between forest and tundra.
recommended_copy.src/engine/field-requests.ts treeline-hi: Start in Treeline Pass; log stone-lift, lee-watch, rime-mark, and talus-hold.

rationale: The current world-map summary uses lower-case high pass language on the Treeline Pass node while High Pass is also the next route title.
The current High Pass route summary says clues go into High Pass, which is readable but less precise than starting from Treeline Pass.
Two exact-copy changes keep travel intent clearer without adding tutorial prose or changing map/station behavior.
preserve: High Pass route title and route id
High Pass filed, ready, and active state behavior
route marker behavior, support behavior, save behavior, station state, world-map focus, world-map walking, corridor geometry, and field-request progression

result: Updated Treeline Pass's world-map footer summary to Wind-bent edge between forest and tundra.
Updated the active treeline-high-pass route summary to Start in Treeline Pass; log stone-lift, lee-watch, rime-mark, and talus-hold.
Added focused exact-copy coverage while preserving labels, ids, route/state behavior, support behavior, save behavior, world-map behavior, station state, and corridor geometry.

lane_2_critic_result.result: Clean review. The Treeline Pass map footer and active High Pass route summary clarify place-vs-route language without changing route identity, map labels, route state, support, save, station, world-map focus/walking, corridor geometry, or field-request progression.
confirmed: Treeline Pass, HIGH PASS MAP, HIGH PASS, the High Pass route title, and the treeline-high-pass route id remain unchanged.
Only the Treeline world-map summary and the active treeline-high-pass route summary changed for this packet's runtime scope.
Focused exact-copy coverage now protects both lines.
No map tutorial paragraph, HUD, station panel, route objective, save shape, corridor behavior, map-return-label rewrite, or approach-label rewrite was added.

lane_4_scout_refinement.target: Assert route-marker and world-map replay behavior across active, ready-to-file, filed, replay-locator, and no-active-route states without adding a new surface.
existing_coverage: Field-request state already covers season-close outing locator, active post-season High Pass, and filed High Pass suppression as separate tests.
Save snapshots cover active High Pass, ready-to-file High Pass station filing state, filed High Pass, and filed High Pass with route-marker selected.
Runtime smoke has broad full-flow coverage from active route-marker High Pass through ready-to-file return and filed suppression.
Controller coverage confirms route-marker remains map-facing and does not bias notebook chip or inspect target behavior.
recommended_scope: Add the smallest guard in src/engine/field-request-state.ts so routeV2.status === 'ready-to-synthesize' does not synthesize an activeOuting.
Keep ready-to-file activeFieldRequest and journalFieldRequest intact so notebook and station filing remain visible.
Do not fall through to resolveSeasonOutingLocator(save) for an active ready-to-file request.
Add one focused src/test/field-requests.test.ts state-matrix test covering active High Pass, ready-to-file High Pass, season-close replay locator, and filed High Pass with route-marker selected.
acceptance: Active High Pass with owned and selected route-marker still marks Treeline Pass and shows Today: High Pass on the focused treeline map node.
Ready-to-file High Pass keeps activeFieldRequest and journalFieldRequest for treeline-high-pass, but activeOuting, routeMarkerLocationId, and routeReplayLabel are null.
The season-close replay locator with no active field request still creates route-locator:treeline journal state and Today: High Pass replay label.
Filed High Pass with selected route-marker stays fully suppressed: no active request, active outing, journal request, route marker, or replay label.

result: Added a ready-to-file Route v2 guard so active notebook-ready requests keep activeFieldRequest and journalFieldRequest but do not synthesize activeOuting.
Because activeOuting is null for ready-to-file requests, route-marker and world-map replay labels stay suppressed while the station-side filing step is active.
Added focused High Pass state-matrix coverage for active route-marker, ready-to-file, season-close locator, and filed route-marker states.

lane_4_critic_result.result: Clean review. The ready-to-file Route v2 guard keeps notebook filing state alive while suppressing active outing, route-marker, and world-map replay affordances.
confirmed: Ready-to-file requests remain activeFieldRequest and journalFieldRequest.
Ready-to-file requests do not synthesize activeOuting and do not fall through to the season outing locator.
Active High Pass with selected route-marker still marks treeline and shows Today: High Pass.
The season-close no-active-field-request locator still creates route-locator:treeline and Today: High Pass.
Filed High Pass with selected route-marker remains suppressed.
lane_4_critic_result.lane_4_status: packet 143 lane-4 chain clear; promote ECO-20260420-scout-385
